entries.claim: Session V2 round-trips only bounded visible user, assistant, source, and terminal records.
The authoritative OneDrive manifest is encrypted and authenticated rather than uploaded as plaintext JSON.
One fenced manifest CAS advances visible, request, and UUID-binding heads together.
A direct request-key miss still consults the authoritative UUID index before accepting a new request.
Reusing one request UUID for another route is rejected from the durable manifest index.
Concurrent product-session index updates are serialized as one read-modify-write operation without lost entries.
A mutable product request persists its receipt across restart, replays one response, and rejects semantic UUID reuse.
A quiet provider turn renews its authoritative session lease without waiting for stream events.
A conflicted lease guard reloads and clears only its exact authoritative lease.
A stale guard release cannot clear a replacement holder's lease.
Recovery rejects every provider generation, policy, harness, installation, provider, or model authority change.
The host emits done complete only after durable terminal publication.
Turn cancellation before persistence discards late provider output.
Pairing claim, local installation, and remote finalization resume idempotently across restart boundaries.
Strict JSON rejects recursive duplicate members and trailing input.
Mutation-intent commit verifies total length, digest, expiry, and binding before an effect.
Session and user-presence routes require trusted session, Agent capability, strict JSON, and no-store responses.
The desktop and mobile Assistant surfaces pass 61 setup, model, source, confirmation, cancellation, autoscroll, and redaction assertions.
Real Claude and ChatGPT product OAuth each completed a read-only StoreArchive turn with at least one source and no local-CLI fallback.
entries.command: jq -e '.result == "pass" and .claude.read_only_storearchive_turn == "complete" and .codex.read_only_storearchive_turn == "complete" and .runtime_to_implementation_diff_is_cfg_test_only == true and .local_cli_origin_used == false' docs/evidence/artifacts/issue-628/pre-rc/provider-harness-matrix.json
entries.notes: The live turns ran at 018dfa5255271f802d3ea05b96b396bec2662c66. The only later change in the pinned implementation commit is a cfg(test)-only mutex in crates/core/src/envelope.rs; the exact implementation APK was rebuilt and installed afterward.

entries.claim: The exact implementation commit produced a clean default APK, excluded hook and experimental markers, installed successfully, and retained connected product state.
entries.command: jq -e '.result == "pass" and .implementation_commit == "4df8d89ec2d71c838e234b2a0d9388eb4412c59c" and .connected == true and .failed_runs == 0 and .network_hook_marker == false and .experimental_subscription_marker == false' docs/evidence/artifacts/issue-628/pre-rc/default-apk-matrix.json

entries.claim: Product feature graphs include OneDrive session transport while excluding experimental authentication and default-off device hooks.
entries.command: jq -e '.result == "pass" and .product_agent_onedrive_transport == true and .default_daemon_experimental_subscription == false and .default_mobile_test_hooks == false' docs/evidence/artifacts/issue-628/pre-rc/feature-graphs.json

entries.claim: The exact implementation commit passes workspace tests, workspace Clippy, format, dependency policy, workflow lint, probe tests, UI smoke, traceability, and boundary scans.
entries.command: cargo remote -c -- test --workspace --no-fail-fast && cargo remote -c -- clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo-remote-fmt --check && cargo deny check && actionlint

entries.claim: The merged account-lifecycle baseline remains valid and current recovery rejects stale credential generations.
Controlled confirmation and cleanup evidence records zero unconfirmed, duplicate, or unreverted cloud effects and no identity data.
